and the laundry.
oh, yes, the laundry.

the pile that i created and then jumped into. she fell with me and found the soft blankets. and napped there. i napped there. it is a rainy and cold day. the pile was welcoming.

but there is always. always. the laundry. the perpetual laundry. we are born in it. we eat and sleep in it. it

text:
the invitation to pause. the invitation to stop. an invitation that is ignored.
“to speak means taking action.” ~ judith butler “any words which touch upon religion, the law or sexual desire are guaranteed to offend someone.” ~ walter savage landor
why then, why then are we so surprised when we are offended? why then do we not consider the possibility when we post that someone might disagree? why then do we not understand the human tendency to take offense, to fight for the right to our interpretation of events? if we are to risk being human, then we are to risk taking offense.

prompt_text: the spreadsheet. oh, the spreadsheet. the spreadsheet that scrolls and scrolls and scrolls. cell beneath cell beneath cell. the column that sums the column. enter and enter and enter. the row continues into the row. it is never finished. it is always open. the spreadsheet. the spreadsheet.
text:
the spreadsheet.

i work with spreadsheets every day, all day. they document and collect data and provide support to complicated tasks. they can be useful, beautiful, and good. today i am feeling angry and frustrated by spreadsheets. i cannot find the bugs. i cannot find the typos. i can’t see clearly how the data is used in formulas. i cannot move forward without tackling the spreadsheets. and i cannot tackle the spreadsheets without feeling fatigued, demoralized, and agitated.

and so. it is time for a break. and so. here i am. writing. and finding some relief in words. and finding some peace
